commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.review_summary:
The branch changes the Feishu client HTTP adapter and tests so Lark SDK REST calls and WS bootstrap requests use OpenClaw's ambient proxy agent with axios proxy auto-detection disabled.

PR surface: Source +18, Tests +82. Total +100 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level: current main delegates Feishu SDK REST and WS bootstrap HTTP to axios-backed Lark HTTP instances, and axios uses proxy env unless config.proxy is false. The PR body also reports a real WSL2 gateway/probe recovery after applying the equivalent installed-plugin hotfix.
commands.review_followup: A narrow automated repair can adjust the Feishu HTTP adapter and tests to honor managed-proxy agent override before maintainer merge review.

target.title: fix(feishu): route SDK HTTP through ambient proxy
target.body:
AI-assisted contribution.

## Summary

- Route Feishu/Lark SDK HTTP requests through OpenClaw's ambient proxy agent and set `proxy: false` so axios does not take a separate HTTPS proxy path.
- Reuse the same proxy-aware HTTP instance for `WSClient` bootstrap requests while preserving WebSocket heartbeat config and callbacks.
- Preserve request-level `httpAgent`/`httpsAgent` values and add tests for HTTP API proxying, WS bootstrap proxying, and caller-provided agents.

Fixes #48949

## Verification

- `pnpm install`
- `pnpm test extensions/feishu/src/client.test.ts`
- `pnpm format:check extensions/feishu/src/client.ts extensions/feishu/src/client.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`
- `pnpm test:extension feishu`
- `pnpm check:changed`
- `codex review --base origin/main` (twice; no actionable regressions found)

## Real Behavior Proof

Behavior addressed: Feishu token/API and WebSocket bootstrap failed when the gateway process had `HTTP_PROXY`/`HTTPS_PROXY` configured, producing `tenant_access_token` failures and 400 responses containing "The plain HTTP request was sent to HTTPS port".

Real environment tested: WSL2 Ubuntu host running OpenClaw 2026.5.22 with the systemd user gateway and `HTTP_PROXY`/`HTTPS_PROXY`/`OPENCLAW_PROXY_URL=http://127.0.0.1:7890`. To avoid replacing this source checkout into my live gateway, I first applied the equivalent dist hotfix to the installed Feishu plugin, restarted the gateway, then ported the same fix to source in this PR.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: `systemctl --user restart openclaw-gateway`; `openclaw channels status --channel feishu --deep --probe`; `journalctl --user -u openclaw-gateway --since '2026-05-25 15:12:04' --no-pager | rg 'Request failed with status code 400|connect failed|unable to connect|socket hang up|tenant_access_token|bot identity|ws client ready|bot open_id resolved'`

Evidence after fix: The probe reported `Feishu default: enabled, configured, running, works`; gateway logs showed `bot open_id resolved` and `[ws] ws client ready`, with no matching 400/connect-failed/token errors after the final restart.

Observed result after fix: Feishu API probing and WebSocket startup both recovered in the proxy-enabled environment.

What was not tested: I did not swap this source checkout into the live gateway or run the repository-wide `pnpm build && pnpm check && pnpm test`; this PR includes targeted source tests, `test:extension feishu`, `check:changed`, and live validation through the equivalent installed-package hotfix.
target.author: larry-xue
